_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Framber Valdez suspended five games for intentionally hitting Trevor Story
items.snark: Valdez threw his first four seam fastball of the season directly at Story's spine, totally accidental guys.

items.headline: Red Sox vs Rays preview: Jake Bennett gets second start after strong debut
items.snark: Bennett threw five innings of one run ball in his debut, which by 2026 Sox standards is damn near Cy Young territory.

items.headline: Rays riding six game winning streak into Fenway Park series opener
items.snark: Sox are 16-21, Rays are 24-12 and scorching hot, but sure this is the homestand where everything turns around.

items.headline: Red Sox Hall of Fame class includes Damon, Lester, Timlin to be honored tonight
items.snark: Finally honoring guys who actually knew how to win games, unlike the current roster construction.

items.headline: Sonny Gray returns from hamstring injury with five shutout innings in sweep clincher
items.snark: Gray threw 70 pitches and avoided injury, which counts as a rousing success for this snake-bitten rotation.

items.headline: Interim manager Chad Tracy now 6-4 since taking over for fired Alex Cora
items.snark: Tracy's doing better than Cora did, which tells you everything about how cursed April was for this squad.

items.headline: Red Sox sweep Tigers 4-0 behind Gray's return, head home on three game win streak
items.snark: Sox won with four hits and 15 strikeouts, proving that in 2026 up is down and black is white.

items.headline: Red Sox offense finally shows life in Detroit sweep after month of anemic hitting
items.snark: They tallied 14 runs over three games, which is apparently what passes for an offensive explosion in 2026.

items.headline: Patriots 2026 strength of schedule ranks 12th easiest, tougher than 2025
items.snark: Pats had the NFL's easiest schedule in 2025 and made the Super Bowl, now they face actual competition.

items.headline: Drake Maye experiencing no problems at all after late season shoulder injury
items.snark: Maye's shoulder is fine and he's not distracted by the Vrabel scandal, everything is totally normal here.

items.headline: Patriots rookie minicamp set for May 8-10 with 12 UDFAs joining nine draft picks
items.snark: Twenty one rookies will show up to learn the playbook and realize NFL speed is very different from college.
